2604.10557.title: LLMs Should Incorporate Explicit Mechanisms for Human Empathy
2604.10557.paper_summary: This paper argues that Large Language Models (LLMs) should incorporate explicit mechanisms for human empathy. As LLMs become increasingly deployed in high-stakes human-centered settings, their success depends not only on correctness or fluency but on faithful preservation of human perspectives. Yet, current LLMs systematically fail at this requirement: even when well-aligned and policy-compliant, they often attenuate affect, misrepresent contextual salience, and rigidify relational stance in ways that distort meaning. We formalize empathy as an observable behavioral property: the capacity to model and respond to human perspectives while preserving intention, affect, and context. Under this framing, we identify four recurring mechanisms of empathic failure in contemporary LLMs--sentiment attenuation, empathic granularity mismatch, conflict avoidance, and linguistic distancing--arising as structural consequences of prevailing training and alignment practices. We further organize these failures along three dimensions: cognitive, cultural, and relational empathy, to explain their manifestation across tasks. Empirical analyses show that strong benchmark performance can mask systematic empathic distortions, motivating empathy-aware objectives, benchmarks, and training signals as first-class components of LLM development.
2604.10557.paper_summary_zh: 這篇論文主張大型語言模型（LLMs）應該納入明確的人類同理心機制。隨著LLMs在高風險以人為中心的環境中越來越多地被部署，它們的成功不僅取決於正確性或流暢性，還取決於忠實保留人類視角。然而，當前的LLMs在這一要求上系統性失敗：即使在良好對齊和符合政策的情況下，它們經常減弱情感、錯誤表述上下文的重要性，並以扭曲意義的方式僵化關係立場。我們將同理心形式化為一種可觀察的行為特徵：即在保留意圖、情感和上下文的同時，建模和回應人類視角的能力。在這一框架下，我們確定當代LLMs中同理心失敗的四種重複機制——情感減弱、同理心細緻度不匹配、衝突迴避和語言疏離——這些都是當前訓練和對齊實踐的結構性後果。我們進一步沿著三個維度組織這些失敗：認知、文化和關係同理心，以解釋它們在各任務中的表現。實證分析顯示，強大的基準表現可能掩蓋系統性的同理心扭曲，這促使我們將同理心意識的目標、基準和訓練信號作為LLM開發的第一級組成部分。
2604.10557.author: Xiaoxing You et.al.
2604.10557.authors: Xiaoxing You, Qiang Huang, Jun Yu
